name: Rust API Endpoint Checks
summary: Review and test checks for exposing a use case through a Rust Axum HTTP endpoint.

checks: When a Cargo workspace or package is present, run cargo check, cargo fmt --check, and cargo test; report why for any check that is not feasible.
Confirm the handler delegates to an existing application/use-case boundary before implementing; if not, report the missing prerequisite instead of creating business behavior.
Review that the handler does not contain persistence queries or domain decisions.
Verify expected success and error responses are covered by tests or explicit review.

items.text: Route is registered and uses the intended HTTP method and path.
An existing application/use-case boundary owns the behavior before the Axum adapter is added; if absent, the missing prerequisite is reported and no business behavior is created.
Handler delegates to the existing use-case boundary.
Request DTO validation or parsing failures return intentional client errors.
Application errors are mapped to stable HTTP responses.
cargo check, cargo fmt --check, and cargo test were run when a Cargo workspace or package is present, or the reason each infeasible command could not run is recorded.
